revision.text:
{{Italic title|reason=}} {{Nihongo|'''''Rikkokushi'''''|六国史|}} is a general term for Japan's '''Six National Histories''' chronicling the mythology and history of Japan from the earliest times to 887.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref> The six histories were written at the imperial court during the 8th and 9th centuries, under order of the Emperors.<ref>Sakamoto (1991)</ref> The basic sources were the court records kept by the Ministry of Central Imperial Affairs, and the biographies of meritorious officials composed in the Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref>
The collection consists of the following texts: *''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicle of Japan; also called ''Nihongi'') – 30 volumes covering the mythological period through 697. Completed by [[Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and [[Sugano no Mamichi]] in 797. *''[[Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by [[Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] and [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]] in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by [[Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]], [[Fujiwara no Yoshimi]], [[Tomo no Yoshio]], and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] and [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]] in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]] and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.
The national histories were discontinued after the ''Sandai Jitsuroku''; they were followed by the [[Shikyō|four Mirror books]] (starting with ''[[Ōkagami]]'').
==See also== *''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories'' *[[Historiography of Japan]]
==Notes== <references/>
==References== *{{Cite book | editor = Kōdansha | title = Kodansha Encyclopedia of Japan | publisher = Kōdansha | year = 1983 | id = }} *{{Cite book | last = Sakamoto | first = Tarō |author2=tr. John S. Brownlee | title = The Six National Histories of Japan | publisher = UBC Press, University of Tokyo Press | year = 1991 | isbn = 0-7748-0379-7 }}
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/sheet/rikkoku.html Full text] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www013.upp.so-net.ne.jp/wata/rikkokusi/ Full text] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Rikkokushi}}

[[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1151168]] Wikidata name: Rikkokushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Italic title|reason=}} {{Nihongo|'''''Rikkokushi'''''|六国史|}} is a general term for Japan's '''Six National Histories''' chronicling the mythology and history of Japan from the earliest times to 887.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref> The six histories were written at the imperial court during the 8th and 9th centuries, under order of the Emperors.<ref>Sakamoto (1991)</ref> The basic sources were the court records kept by the Ministry of Central Imperial Affairs, and the biographies of meritorious officials composed in the Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref>
The collection consists of the following texts: *''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicle of Japan; also called ''Nihongi'') – 30 volumes covering the mythological period through 697. Completed by [[:en:Toneri Shinnō|Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and [[:en:Sugano no Mamichi|Sugano no Mamichi]] in 797. *''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Otsugu|Fujiwara no Otsugu]] in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshimi|Fujiwara no Yoshimi]], [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] and [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]] in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]] and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.
The national histories were discontinued after the ''Sandai Jitsuroku''; they were followed by the [[:en:Shikyō|four Mirror books]] (starting with ''[[:en:Ōkagami|Ōkagami]]'').
==See also== *''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories'' *[[:en:Historiography of Japan|Historiography of Japan]]
==Notes== <references/>
==References== *{{Cite book | editor = Kōdansha | title = Kodansha Encyclopedia of Japan | publisher = Kōdansha | year = 1983 | id = }} *{{Cite book | last = Sakamoto | first = Tarō |author2=tr. John S. Brownlee | title = The Six National Histories of Japan | publisher = UBC Press, University of Tokyo Press | year = 1991 | isbn = 0-7748-0379-7 }}
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/sheet/rikkoku.html Full text] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www013.upp.so-net.ne.jp/wata/rikkokusi/ Full text] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Rikkokushi}}

The collection consists of the following texts: *''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicle of Japan; also called ''Nihongi'') – 30 volumes covering the mythological period through 697. Completed by [[:en:Toneri Shinnō|Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and [[:en:Sugano no Mamichi|Sugano no Mamichi]] in 797. *''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Otsugu|Fujiwara no Otsugu]] in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshimi|Fujiwara no Yoshimi]], [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] and [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]] in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]] and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.
The national histories were discontinued after the ''Sandai Jitsuroku''; they were followed by the [[:en:Shikyō|four Mirror books]] (starting with ''[[:en:Ōkagami|Ōkagami]]'').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1151168]] Wikidata name: Rikkokushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Italic title|reason=}} {{Nihongo|'''''Rikkokushi'''''|六国史|}} is a general term for Japan's '''Six National Histories''' chronicling the mythology and history of Japan from the earliest times to 887.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref> The six histories were written at the imperial court during the 8th and 9th centuries, under order of the Emperors.<ref>Sakamoto (1991)</ref> The basic sources were the court records kept by the Ministry of Central Imperial Affairs, and the biographies of meritorious officials composed in the Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref>
The collection consists of the following texts: *''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicle of Japan; also called ''Nihongi'') – 30 volumes covering the mythological period through 697. Completed by [[:en:Toneri Shinnō|Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and [[:en:Sugano no Mamichi|Sugano no Mamichi]] in 797. *''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Otsugu|Fujiwara no Otsugu]] in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshimi|Fujiwara no Yoshimi]], [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] and [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]] in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]] and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.
The national histories were discontinued after the ''Sandai Jitsuroku''; they were followed by the [[:en:Shikyō|four Mirror books]] (starting with ''[[:en:Ōkagami|Ōkagami]]'').
==See also== *''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories'' *[[:en:Historiography of Japan|Historiography of Japan]]

Completed by [[:en:Toneri Shinnō|Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and [[:en:Sugano no Mamichi|Sugano no Mamichi]] in 797. *''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Otsugu|Fujiwara no Otsugu]] in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshimi|Fujiwara no Yoshimi]], [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] and [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]] in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]] and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.

revision.comment: Bot: reorganize categories (alphabetical, deduplicated, moved to bottom)
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1151168]] Wikidata name: Rikkokushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Italic title|reason=}} {{Nihongo|'''''Rikkokushi'''''|六国史|}} is a general term for Japan's '''Six National Histories''' chronicling the mythology and history of Japan from the earliest times to 887.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref> The six histories were written at the imperial court during the 8th and 9th centuries, under order of the Emperors.<ref>Sakamoto (1991)</ref> The basic sources were the court records kept by the Ministry of Central Imperial Affairs, and the biographies of meritorious officials composed in the Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref>
The collection consists of the following texts: *''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicle of Japan; also called ''Nihongi'') – 30 volumes covering the mythological period through 697. Completed by [[:en:Toneri Shinnō|Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and [[:en:Sugano no Mamichi|Sugano no Mamichi]] in 797. *''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Otsugu|Fujiwara no Otsugu]] in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshimi|Fujiwara no Yoshimi]], [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] and [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]] in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]] and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.
The national histories were discontinued after the ''Sandai Jitsuroku''; they were followed by the [[:en:Shikyō|four Mirror books]] (starting with ''[[:en:Ōkagami|Ōkagami]]'').
==See also== *''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories'' *[[:en:Historiography of Japan|Historiography of Japan]]
==Notes== <references/>
==References== *{{Cite book | editor = Kōdansha | title = Kodansha Encyclopedia of Japan | publisher = Kōdansha | year = 1983 | id = }} *{{Cite book | last = Sakamoto | first = Tarō |author2=tr. John S. Brownlee | title = The Six National Histories of Japan | publisher = UBC Press, University of Tokyo Press | year = 1991 | isbn = 0-7748-0379-7 }}
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/sheet/rikkoku.html Full text] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www013.upp.so-net.ne.jp/wata/rikkokusi/ Full text] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1151168]] Wikidata name: Rikkokushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Italic title|reason=}} {{Nihongo|'''''Rikkokushi'''''|六国史|}} is a general term for Japan's '''Six National Histories''' chronicling the mythology and history of Japan from the earliest times to 887.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref> The six histories were written at the imperial court during the 8th and 9th centuries, under order of the Emperors.<ref>Sakamoto (1991)</ref> The basic sources were the court records kept by the Ministry of Central Imperial Affairs, and the biographies of meritorious officials composed in the Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref>
The collection consists of the following texts: *''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicle of Japan; also called ''Nihongi'') – 30 volumes covering the mythological period through 697. Completed by [[:en:Toneri Shinnō|Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and [[:en:Sugano no Mamichi|Sugano no Mamichi]] in 797. *''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Otsugu|Fujiwara no Otsugu]] in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshimi|Fujiwara no Yoshimi]], [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] and [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]] in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]] and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.
The national histories were discontinued after the ''Sandai Jitsuroku''; they were followed by the [[:en:Shikyō|four Mirror books]] (starting with ''[[:en:Ōkagami|Ōkagami]]'').
==See also== *''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories'' *[[:en:Historiography of Japan|Historiography of Japan]]
==Notes== <references/>
==References== *{{Cite book | editor = Kōdansha | title = Kodansha Encyclopedia of Japan | publisher = Kōdansha | year = 1983 | id = }} *{{Cite book | last = Sakamoto | first = Tarō |author2=tr. John S. Brownlee | title = The Six National Histories of Japan | publisher = UBC Press, University of Tokyo Press | year = 1991 | isbn = 0-7748-0379-7 }}
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/sheet/rikkoku.html Full text] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www013.upp.so-net.ne.jp/wata/rikkokusi/ Full text] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Rikkokushi}}
[[en:Rikkokushi]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Rikkokuši]] [[de:Rikkokushi]] [[es:Rikkokushi]] [[eu:Rikkokushi]] [[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]] [[fr:Rikkokushi]] [[it:Rikkokushi]] [[ja:六国史]] [[ko:육국사]] [[pt:Rikkokushi]] [[ru:Риккокуси]] [[simple:Rikkokushi]] [[zh:六國史]]

Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|en|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|WD=Q3090616|lt=Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu}} in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|en|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|WD=Q960841|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshifusa}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshimi|en|Fujiwara no Yoshimi|WD=Q11623503|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshimi}}, {{ill|Tomo no Yoshio|en|Tomo no Yoshio|WD=Q7820079|lt=Tomo no Yoshio}}, and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1151168]] Wikidata name: Rikkokushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Italic title|reason=}} {{Nihongo|'''''Rikkokushi'''''|六国史|}} is a general term for Japan's '''Six National Histories''' chronicling the mythology and history of Japan from the earliest times to 887.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref> The six histories were written at the imperial court during the 8th and 9th centuries, under order of the Emperors.<ref>Sakamoto (1991)</ref> The basic sources were the court records kept by the Ministry of Central Imperial Affairs, and the biographies of meritorious officials composed in the Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref>
The collection consists of the following texts: *''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicle of Japan; also called ''Nihongi'') – 30 volumes covering the mythological period through 697. Completed by [[:en:Toneri Shinnō|Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and {{ill|Sugano no Mamichi|en|Sugano no Mamichi|WD=Q11619464|lt=Sugano no Mamichi}} in 797. *''{{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki}}'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|en|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|WD=Q3090616|lt=Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu}} in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|en|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|WD=Q960841|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshifusa}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshimi|en|Fujiwara no Yoshimi|WD=Q11623503|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshimi}}, {{ill|Tomo no Yoshio|en|Tomo no Yoshio|WD=Q7820079|lt=Tomo no Yoshio}}, and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Mototsune|en|Fujiwara no Mototsune|WD=Q1397196|lt=Fujiwara no Mototsune}} and {{ill|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|en|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|WD=Q11619187|lt=Sugawara no Koreyoshi}} in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.
The national histories were discontinued after the ''Sandai Jitsuroku''; they were followed by the {{ill|Shikyō|en|Shikyō|WD=Q2278822|lt=four Mirror books}} (starting with ''{{ill|Ōkagami|en|Ōkagami|WD=Q385120|lt=Ōkagami}}'').
==See also== *''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories'' *{{ill|Historiography of Japan|en|Historiography of Japan|WD=Q5774518|lt=Historiography of Japan}}
==Notes== <references/>
==References== *{{Cite book | editor = Kōdansha | title = Kodansha Encyclopedia of Japan | publisher = Kōdansha | year = 1983 | id = }} *{{Cite book | last = Sakamoto | first = Tarō |author2=tr. John S. Brownlee | title = The Six National Histories of Japan | publisher = UBC Press, University of Tokyo Press | year = 1991 | isbn = 0-7748-0379-7 }}
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/sheet/rikkoku.html Full text] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www013.upp.so-net.ne.jp/wata/rikkokusi/ Full text] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Rikkokushi}}

Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and {{ill|Sugano no Mamichi|en|Sugano no Mamichi|WD=Q11619464|lt=Sugano no Mamichi}} in 797. *''{{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki}}'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|en|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|WD=Q3090616|lt=Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu}} in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|en|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|WD=Q960841|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshifusa}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshimi|en|Fujiwara no Yoshimi|WD=Q11623503|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshimi}}, {{ill|Tomo no Yoshio|en|Tomo no Yoshio|WD=Q7820079|lt=Tomo no Yoshio}}, and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Mototsune|en|Fujiwara no Mototsune|WD=Q1397196|lt=Fujiwara no Mototsune}} and {{ill|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|en|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|WD=Q11619187|lt=Sugawara no Koreyoshi}} in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Rikkokuši]][[de:Rikkokushi]][[en:Rikkokushi]][[es:Rikkokushi]][[eu:Rikkokushi]][[fa:شش تاریخ ملی]][[fr:Rikkokushi]][[it:Rikkokushi]][[ja:六国史]][[ko:육국사]][[pt:Rikkokushi]][[ru:Риккокуси]][[simple:Rikkokushi]][[zh:六國史]]
{{Italic title|reason=}} {{Nihongo|'''''Rikkokushi'''''|六国史|}} is a general term for Japan's '''Six National Histories''' chronicling the mythology and history of Japan from the earliest times to 887.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref> The six histories were written at the imperial court during the 8th and 9th centuries, under order of the Emperors.<ref>Sakamoto (1991)</ref> The basic sources were the court records kept by the Ministry of Central Imperial Affairs, and the biographies of meritorious officials composed in the Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs.<ref>Kōdansha (1983)</ref>
The collection consists of the following texts: *''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicle of Japan; also called ''Nihongi'') – 30 volumes covering the mythological period through 697. Completed by [[:en:Toneri Shinnō|Toneri Shinnō]] in 720. *''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'' (Chronicle of Japan, Continued; also called ''Shokki'') – 40 volumes covering 697 through 791. Completed by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tsugutada|Fujiwara no Tsugutada]] and {{ill|Sugano no Mamichi|en|Sugano no Mamichi|WD=Q11619464|lt=Sugano no Mamichi|1=Sugano no Mamichi}} in 797. *''{{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki|1=Nihon Kōki}}'' (Later Chronicle of Japan) – 40 volumes covering 792 through 833. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|en|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|WD=Q3090616|lt=Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|1=Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu|1=Fujiwara no Otsugu}} in 840. *''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (Later Chronicle of Japan, Continued) – 20 volumes covering 833 through 850. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|en|Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|WD=Q960841|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshifusa|1=Fujiwara no Yoshifusa}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshimi|en|Fujiwara no Yoshimi|WD=Q11623503|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshimi|1=Fujiwara no Yoshimi}}, {{ill|Tomo no Yoshio|en|Tomo no Yoshio|WD=Q7820079|lt=Tomo no Yoshio|1=Tomo no Yoshio}}, and [[Haruzumi no Yoshitada]] in 869. *''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Emperor Montoku of Japan; also called ''Montoku jitsuroku'') – 10 volumes covering 850 through 858. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Mototsune|en|Fujiwara no Mototsune|WD=Q1397196|lt=Fujiwara no Mototsune|1=Fujiwara no Mototsune}} and {{ill|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|en|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|WD=Q11619187|lt=Sugawara no Koreyoshi|1=Sugawara no Koreyoshi}} in 879. *''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Veritable Record of Three Generations [of Emperors] of Japan; also called ''Sandai jitsuroku'') – 50 volumes covering 858 through 887. Completed by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]] in 901.
The national histories were discontinued after the ''Sandai Jitsuroku''; they were followed by the {{ill|Shikyō|en|Shikyō|WD=Q2278822|lt=four Mirror books|1=Shikyō}} (starting with ''{{ill|Ōkagami|en|Ōkagami|WD=Q385120|lt=Ōkagami|1=Ōkagami}}'').
==See also== *''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories'' *{{ill|Historiography of Japan|en|Historiography of Japan|WD=Q5774518|lt=Historiography of Japan|1=Historiography of Japan}}
==Notes== <references/>
==References== *{{Cite book | editor = Kōdansha | title = Kodansha Encyclopedia of Japan | publisher = Kōdansha | year = 1983 | id = }} *{{Cite book | last = Sakamoto | first = Tarō |author2=tr. John S. Brownlee | title = The Six National Histories of Japan | publisher = UBC Press, University of Tokyo Press | year = 1991 | isbn = 0-7748-0379-7 }}
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/sheet/rikkoku.html Full text] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www013.upp.so-net.ne.jp/wata/rikkokusi/ Full text] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Rikkokushi}}
